notes: Pose estimation from Hailo Model Zoo (yolov8m_pose). PERSON BOUNDING BOXES ONLY in this build - the actual skeleton (joints + connecting lines) does NOT render. function_name MUST be `filter`, not `yolov8`: the .so's `yolov8(HailoROIPtr)` symbol returns std::pair<vector<KeyPt>, vector<PairPairs>>, not void; hailofilter calls the named entrypoint as `void(HailoROIPtr)`, so calling `yolov8` mismatches the AArch64 ABI return-slot pointer and corrupts the caller's stack. `filter` is the void wrapper at libyolov8pose_post.so:405 that delegates to yolov8 with the correct signature. Why no skeleton: hailooverlay's HAILO_LANDMARKS dispatch on hailo-tappas-core 5.1.0 renders nothing; AND the .so's UINT16 keypoint output from this HEF is not dequantized to valid bbox-relative coords (every keypoint comes back at confidence=0.00 with off-screen coordinates). Lifting per-detection landmarks to ROI top level, a custom OpenCV-backed poseoverlay plugin, and forcing hailonet output-format-type=float32/uint8 all confirmed the keypoint data is unusable. A working skeleton would require writing a YOLOv8-pose post-processor from scratch (DFL box regression + UINT16 keypoint dequant + NMS) - deferred. Boxes alone are still useful as a person-presence cue.
